report_name: Ransomware Turf War: 0APT and KryBit Groups Hack Each Other in Public Feud, Leaking Ops Data

threat_actors.name: 0APT
threat_actors.description: An amateurish ransomware group that initiated a public feud by leaking data from the rival group KryBit. In retaliation, 0APT was hacked, its leak site defaced, and its operational data was dumped, revealing it had fabricated over 190 victim listings and was running its infrastructure on an Android phone.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware, although it was revealed they fabricated most of their victim claims.

threat_actors.name: KryBit
threat_actors.description: A lower-tier ransomware group that retaliated against the 0APT group by hacking their infrastructure, defacing their leak site, and leaking their entire operational dataset. The group reportedly had five affiliates and around 20 potential victims with ransom demands between $40,000 and $100,000.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware, with demands ranging from $40,000 to $100,000.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Used by both 0APT and KryBit to breach each other's web-based leak sites and admin panels during their public feud.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Private Repositories
attack_patterns.description: Used by both 0APT and KryBit to collect and leak sensitive operational data from each other's compromised backend infrastructure and admin panels.

attack_patterns.name: Defacement
attack_patterns.description: Used by KryBit to deface 0APT's data leak site with the message, "Next time, don't play with the big boys."

identities.name: Halcyon
identities.description: A security firm whose expert, Oliver Newbury, commented on the market dynamics and financial pressures within the ransomware ecosystem.

identities.name: Oliver Newbury
identities.description: An expert from the security firm Halcyon who provided commentary on the infighting between 0APT and KryBit, suggesting it indicates financial struggles.

infrastructure.name: KryBit Admin Panel
infrastructure.description: KryBit's administrator panel which was breached and leaked by 0APT, exposing operator details, affiliates, and victim information.

infrastructure.name: 0APT Leak Site
infrastructure.description: 0APT's data leak site, which was defaced by KryBit during their retaliatory attack.

infrastructure.name: 0APT Android Phone Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: The highly amateurish infrastructure used by 0APT to run its operations, which was discovered to be hosted on an Android phone's internal SD card.
